---
title: "Stage 4: Final Phylogeny"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    theme: cosmo
    fig-responsive: true
jupyter: basicpython
params:
  build_dir: "../results/01_build"
  final_dir: "../results/04_final"
  run_name: "pipeline run"
  highlights_file: ""
  min_freq: "0.95"
---

<!--
Render command (use absolute paths — Quarto resolves paths relative to the notebook file):

  conda run -n quarto quarto render \
      /path/to/subspecies-phylogeny/notebooks/stage4_final.qmd \
      -P build_dir:/absolute/path/to/run/results/01_build \
      -P final_dir:/absolute/path/to/run/results/04_final \
      -P run_name:MyRun \
      -P highlights_file:/absolute/path/to/subspecies-phylogeny/notebooks/highlights/MyRun.yml \
      -P min_freq:0.95 \
      --output-dir /absolute/path/to/run/notebooks/

See notebooks/highlights/ for example highlights YAML files and their format.

Tree visualisation: uses toytree + toyplot (installed in basicpython).
  Trees are saved as SVG and interactive HTML via toyplot renderers.

Static image export for Plotly figures (PNG/SVG) requires kaleido:
    mamba install -c conda-forge python-kaleido
  Without kaleido, interactive HTML files are saved instead.
-->

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
#| include: false
# Default parameter values — overridden by -P flags at render time
build_dir       = "../results/01_build"
final_dir       = "../results/04_final"
run_name        = "pipeline run"
highlights_file = ""
min_freq        = "0.95"
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Imports and setup
# ============================================================
import warnings
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import yaml
import toytree
import toyplot
import toyplot.html
import toyplot.svg
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import io
import re

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

build_dir = Path(build_dir)
final_dir = Path(final_dir)
run_name  = str(run_name)
min_freq  = str(min_freq).strip()

figs_dir   = final_dir / "figs"   / "stage4"
tables_dir = final_dir / "tables" / "stage4"
figs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
tables_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

try:
    import kaleido  # noqa: F401
    HAS_KALEIDO = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_KALEIDO = False


def save_fig(fig_plotly, stem):
    if fig_plotly is None:
        return
    fig_plotly.write_html(str(figs_dir / f"{stem}.html"))
    if HAS_KALEIDO:
        fig_plotly.write_image(str(figs_dir / f"{stem}.svg"))
        fig_plotly.write_image(str(figs_dir / f"{stem}.png"), scale=2)


def save_tree(html_str, svg_str, stem):
    (figs_dir / f"{stem}.html").write_text(html_str)
    (figs_dir / f"{stem}.svg").write_text(svg_str)


PLOTLY_LAYOUT = dict(
    template='plotly_white',
    font=dict(family='Arial, sans-serif', size=13),
)
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Load highlights config
# ============================================================
# Default palette cycled when a highlight entry has no explicit color
_DEFAULT_PALETTE = ['#e63946', '#f4a261', '#2a9d8f', '#8338ec', '#fb8500']

highlights = {}   # name -> {'label': str, 'color': str}

_hf = str(highlights_file).strip()
if _hf:
    _path = Path(_hf)
    if _path.exists():
        _cfg = yaml.safe_load(_path.read_text()) or {}
        for i, entry in enumerate(_cfg.get('highlights', [])):
            name  = str(entry['name'])
            label = str(entry.get('label', name))
            color = str(entry.get('color', _DEFAULT_PALETTE[i % len(_DEFAULT_PALETTE)]))
            highlights[name] = {'label': label, 'color': color}
    else:
        import warnings as _w
        _w.warn(f"highlights_file not found: {_path}")


DEFAULT_TIP_COLOR  = '#457b9d'
DEFAULT_TIP_SIZE   = 5
HIGHLIGHT_TIP_SIZE = 9


def tip_color(name):
    return highlights[name]['color'] if name in highlights else DEFAULT_TIP_COLOR


def tip_size(name):
    return HIGHLIGHT_TIP_SIZE if name in highlights else DEFAULT_TIP_SIZE
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Locate files for the chosen min_freq
# ============================================================
mf_label = f"min_freq_{min_freq}"

iqtree_no_gubbins_dir = final_dir / "iqtree" / "no_gubbins" / mf_label
iqtree_gubbins_dir    = final_dir / "iqtree" / "gubbins"    / mf_label
gubbins_dir           = final_dir / "gubbins" / mf_label

HAS_GUBBINS_TREE = (iqtree_gubbins_dir / "tree.treefile").exists()
HAS_GUBBINS_OUT  = gubbins_dir.exists() and any(gubbins_dir.glob("*.per_branch_statistics.csv"))


def load_iqtree_meta(iqtree_file):
    meta = {}
    if not iqtree_file.exists():
        return meta
    text = iqtree_file.read_text()
    for label, pattern in [
        ('n_sequences',   r'Input data:\s+(\d+) sequences'),
        ('n_sites',       r'Input data:.*with (\d+) nucleotide sites'),
        ('model',         r'Model of substitution:\s+(\S+)'),
        ('log_likelihood',r'Log-likelihood of the tree:\s+([-\d.]+)'),
        ('tree_length',   r'Total tree length \(sum of branch lengths\):\s+([\d.]+)'),
        ('constant_sites',r'Number of constant sites:\s+(\d+)'),
        ('parsimony_inf', r'Number of parsimony informative sites:\s+(\d+)'),
    ]:
        m = re.search(pattern, text)
        if m:
            meta[label] = m.group(1)
    return meta


no_gubbins_meta = load_iqtree_meta(iqtree_no_gubbins_dir / "tree.iqtree")
gubbins_meta    = load_iqtree_meta(iqtree_gubbins_dir    / "tree.iqtree") if HAS_GUBBINS_TREE else {}


def load_gubbins_stats():
    if not HAS_GUBBINS_OUT:
        return None
    csv_files = list(gubbins_dir.glob("*.per_branch_statistics.csv"))
    df = pd.read_csv(csv_files[0], sep='\t')
    return df[~df['Node'].str.match(r'^Node_\d+$', na=False)].copy()


gubbins_stats = load_gubbins_stats()

n_recomb_blocks = 0
if HAS_GUBBINS_OUT:
    gff_files = list(gubbins_dir.glob("*.recombination_predictions.gff"))
    if gff_files:
        with open(gff_files[0]) as fh:
            n_recomb_blocks = sum(1 for l in fh if l.strip() and not l.startswith('#'))
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Tree drawing helpers
# ============================================================

def _tip_style_lists(tt):
    tips = tt.get_tip_labels()
    colors = [tip_color(t) for t in tips]
    sizes  = [tip_size(t)  for t in tips]
    return tips, colors, sizes


def _support_node_style(tt):
    """Per-node colours and sizes for internal bootstrap nodes."""
    tips_set = set(tt.get_tip_labels())
    node_colors, node_sizes = [], []
    node_data = tt.get_node_data()
    has_support = 'support' in node_data.columns

    for i in range(tt.nnodes):
        name = node_data['name'].iloc[i] if 'name' in node_data.columns else ''
        if name in tips_set or i < tt.ntips:
            node_colors.append(tip_color(str(name)))
            node_sizes.append(tip_size(str(name)))
        else:
            sup = node_data['support'].iloc[i] if has_support else None
            if sup is None or pd.isna(sup):
                node_colors.append('#aaaaaa'); node_sizes.append(0)
            elif sup >= 95:
                node_colors.append('#2d6a4f'); node_sizes.append(6)
            elif sup >= 70:
                node_colors.append('#f4a261'); node_sizes.append(5)
            else:
                node_colors.append('#aaaaaa'); node_sizes.append(0)
    return node_colors, node_sizes


def _render_toytree(canvas):
    html_io = io.BytesIO()
    toyplot.html.render(canvas, html_io)
    svg_io  = io.BytesIO()
    toyplot.svg.render(canvas, svg_io)
    return html_io.getvalue().decode(), svg_io.getvalue().decode()


def draw_toytree_with_support(tt, title=None, tip_font_size='10px', width=950):
    tips, tip_colors, _ = _tip_style_lists(tt)
    node_colors, node_sizes = _support_node_style(tt)
    height = max(500, tt.ntips * 14)

    canvas, axes, _ = tt.draw(
        width=width, height=height,
        layout='r', scalebar=True,
        tip_labels=True,
        tip_labels_colors=tip_colors,
        tip_labels_style={'font-size': tip_font_size},
        node_sizes=node_sizes,
        node_colors=node_colors,
        node_style={'stroke': 'none'},
    )
    if title:
        axes.label.text = title
    return _render_toytree(canvas)
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Load and render trees
# ============================================================
tt_no_gubbins = tt_gubbins = None
tt_no_gubbins_html = tt_no_gubbins_svg = ""
tt_gubbins_html    = tt_gubbins_svg    = ""

if (iqtree_no_gubbins_dir / "tree.treefile").exists():
    tt_no_gubbins = toytree.tree(str(iqtree_no_gubbins_dir / "tree.treefile")).ladderize()
    tt_no_gubbins_html, tt_no_gubbins_svg = draw_toytree_with_support(
        tt_no_gubbins,
        title=f"IQ-TREE ML — no Gubbins masking — {run_name} (min_freq={min_freq})"
    )
    save_tree(tt_no_gubbins_html, tt_no_gubbins_svg, "iqtree_no_gubbins")

if HAS_GUBBINS_TREE:
    tt_gubbins = toytree.tree(str(iqtree_gubbins_dir / "tree.treefile")).ladderize()
    tt_gubbins_html, tt_gubbins_svg = draw_toytree_with_support(
        tt_gubbins,
        title=f"IQ-TREE ML — Gubbins-masked — {run_name} (min_freq={min_freq})"
    )
    save_tree(tt_gubbins_html, tt_gubbins_svg, "iqtree_gubbins")
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Run parameters summary table
# ============================================================
highlight_summary = (
    "; ".join(f"{n} ({v['label']})" for n, v in highlights.items())
    if highlights else "none"
)
rows = [
    ("Run name",               run_name),
    ("min-freq",               min_freq),
    ("Sequences",              no_gubbins_meta.get('n_sequences', '—')),
    ("Alignment length",       no_gubbins_meta.get('n_sites', '—')),
    ("Constant sites",         no_gubbins_meta.get('constant_sites', '—')),
    ("Parsimony-inf. sites",   no_gubbins_meta.get('parsimony_inf', '—')),
    ("Substitution model",     no_gubbins_meta.get('model', '—')),
    ("Log-likelihood",         no_gubbins_meta.get('log_likelihood', '—')),
    ("Tree length",            no_gubbins_meta.get('tree_length', '—')),
    ("Gubbins recomb. blocks", str(n_recomb_blocks) if HAS_GUBBINS_OUT else "not run"),
    ("Gubbins-masked IQ-TREE", "yes" if HAS_GUBBINS_TREE else "no / not run"),
    ("Highlighted strains",    highlight_summary),
]
summary_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=["Parameter", "Value"])
summary_df.to_csv(tables_dir / "run_summary.csv", index=False)
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Branch-length distribution
# ============================================================
fig_bl = None
if tt_no_gubbins is not None:
    node_data  = tt_no_gubbins.get_node_data()
    bl_series  = node_data['dist'].dropna()
    bl_series  = bl_series[bl_series > 0]
    tip_labels = set(tt_no_gubbins.get_tip_labels())

    fig_bl = go.Figure()
    fig_bl.add_trace(go.Histogram(
        x=bl_series, nbinsx=60,
        name='All branches',
        marker_color='#457b9d', opacity=0.75,
    ))
    # Vertical lines for each highlighted strain
    if 'name' in node_data.columns:
        for name, info in highlights.items():
            mask = node_data['name'] == name
            if mask.any():
                bl_val = node_data.loc[mask, 'dist'].values[0]
                if bl_val > 0:
                    fig_bl.add_vline(
                        x=bl_val,
                        line_color=info['color'], line_width=2, line_dash='dash',
                        annotation_text=info['label'],
                        annotation_position="top right",
                    )
    fig_bl.update_layout(
        title=f"Branch-length distribution — {run_name}",
        xaxis_title="Branch length (substitutions/site)",
        yaxis_title="Count",
        **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
    )
    save_fig(fig_bl, "branch_lengths")
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Gubbins per-tip statistics
# ============================================================
fig_gubbins = None
if gubbins_stats is not None and len(gubbins_stats) > 0:
    gs = gubbins_stats.sort_values('Total SNPs', ascending=True)
    tip_names  = gs['Node'].tolist()
    bar_colors = [tip_color(t) for t in tip_names]

    fig_gubbins = make_subplots(
        rows=1, cols=2,
        subplot_titles=["Total SNPs per tip", "SNPs in recombinations per tip"],
        horizontal_spacing=0.12,
    )
    fig_gubbins.add_trace(go.Bar(
        y=tip_names, x=gs['Total SNPs'],
        orientation='h', marker_color=bar_colors, name='Total SNPs',
        hovertemplate='%{y}<br>Total SNPs: %{x}<extra></extra>',
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig_gubbins.add_trace(go.Bar(
        y=tip_names, x=gs['Number of SNPs Inside Recombinations'],
        orientation='h', marker_color=bar_colors, name='SNPs in recombinations',
        hovertemplate='%{y}<br>SNPs in recomb: %{x}<extra></extra>',
    ), row=1, col=2)
    fig_gubbins.update_layout(
        height=max(500, len(gs) * 14),
        title=f"Gubbins per-tip statistics — {run_name} (min_freq={min_freq})",
        showlegend=False,
        **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
    )
    fig_gubbins.update_xaxes(title_text="SNPs", row=1, col=1)
    fig_gubbins.update_xaxes(title_text="SNPs", row=1, col=2)
    save_fig(fig_gubbins, "gubbins_per_tip")
    gubbins_stats.to_csv(tables_dir / "gubbins_per_tip.csv", index=False)
```

```{python}
#| include: false
# ============================================================
# Pairwise ML distance heatmap
# ============================================================
fig_dist = None
if (iqtree_no_gubbins_dir / "tree.mldist").exists():
    dist_df = pd.read_csv(
        iqtree_no_gubbins_dir / "tree.mldist",
        sep=r'\s+', header=None, skiprows=1, index_col=0,
    )
    dist_df.columns = dist_df.index

    if tt_no_gubbins is not None:
        leaf_order = tt_no_gubbins.get_tip_labels()
        order = [t for t in leaf_order if t in dist_df.index]
        dist_df = dist_df.reindex(index=order, columns=order)

    tip_names   = list(dist_df.index)
    text_matrix = [[f"Query: {r}<br>Ref: {c}<br>Dist: {dist_df.loc[r,c]:.6f}"
                    for c in tip_names] for r in tip_names]
    ticktext_x  = [f"<b>{t}</b>" if t in highlights else t for t in tip_names]
    ticktext_y  = [f"<b>{t}</b>" if t in highlights else t for t in tip_names]

    fig_dist = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
        z=dist_df.values, x=tip_names, y=tip_names,
        text=text_matrix, hoverinfo='text',
        colorscale='Blues', colorbar=dict(title="ML distance"),
    ))
    fig_dist.update_layout(
        title=f"IQ-TREE pairwise ML distances — {run_name} (min_freq={min_freq})",
        xaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=9), tickangle=45, ticktext=ticktext_x, tickvals=tip_names),
        yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=9), ticktext=ticktext_y, tickvals=tip_names, autorange='reversed'),
        height=800,
        **PLOTLY_LAYOUT,
    )
    save_fig(fig_dist, "pairwise_distances")
    dist_df.to_csv(tables_dir / "pairwise_ml_distances.csv")
```

## Run summary

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import display, HTML

display(HTML(summary_df.to_html(index=False, border=0,
    classes='table table-sm table-striped', justify='left')))
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Highlighted strains** (shown in colour on all trees and charts):
```{python}
#| echo: false
if highlights:
    rows_hl = "".join(
        f'<tr><td><code>{n}</code></td>'
        f'<td><span style="color:{v["color"]};font-weight:bold">■</span> {v["label"]}</td></tr>'
        for n, v in highlights.items()
    )
    display(HTML(f'<table class="table table-sm" style="width:auto">'
                 f'<thead><tr><th>Strain</th><th>Label</th></tr></thead>'
                 f'<tbody>{rows_hl}</tbody></table>'))
else:
    display(HTML("<em>None — pass <code>-P highlights_file:/path/to/highlights.yml</code> to mark strains.</em>"))
```

Bootstrap support on internal nodes:
<span style="color:#2d6a4f; font-weight:bold">■ ≥95</span> &nbsp;
<span style="color:#f4a261; font-weight:bold">■ ≥70</span> &nbsp;
<span style="color:#aaaaaa">■ &lt;70 (not shown)</span>
:::

## 1. IQ-TREE ML phylogeny — no recombination masking

```{python}
#| echo: false
if tt_no_gubbins is not None:
    display(HTML(tt_no_gubbins_html))
else:
    display(HTML(f"<p><em>No IQ-TREE treefile found for min_freq={min_freq} (no_gubbins track).</em></p>"))
```

## 2. IQ-TREE ML phylogeny — Gubbins-masked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if HAS_GUBBINS_TREE and tt_gubbins is not None:
    display(HTML(tt_gubbins_html))
elif HAS_GUBBINS_OUT and not HAS_GUBBINS_TREE:
    display(HTML("""
    <div class="callout callout-style-default callout-note callout-titled">
    <div class="callout-header"><div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
    <div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">No recombination detected</div></div>
    <div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
    <p>Gubbins was run but detected <strong>0 recombination blocks</strong>. No sites were
    masked, so the no-masking tree above is the final tree.</p>
    </div></div>
    """))
else:
    display(HTML("""
    <div class="callout callout-style-default callout-note callout-titled">
    <div class="callout-header"><div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
    <div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">Gubbins not run</div></div>
    <div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
    <p>Gubbins was not run for this dataset (<code>--skip_gubbins</code>).
    Only the unmasked IQ-TREE is available.</p>
    </div></div>
    """))
```

## 3. Branch-length distribution

```{python}
#| echo: false
if fig_bl is not None:
    fig_bl.show()
else:
    display(HTML("<p><em>No tree loaded — branch-length plot unavailable.</em></p>"))
```

## 4. Pairwise ML distances

Heatmap of IQ-TREE pairwise maximum-likelihood distances (`tree.mldist`), ordered by tree leaf order. Highlighted strain labels are **bold**.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if fig_dist is not None:
    fig_dist.show()
else:
    display(HTML("<p><em>No <code>tree.mldist</code> file found.</em></p>"))
```

## 5. Gubbins recombination statistics

```{python}
#| echo: false
if gubbins_stats is not None and len(gubbins_stats) > 0 and n_recomb_blocks > 0:
    fig_gubbins.show()
elif gubbins_stats is not None:
    display(HTML("""
    <div class="callout callout-style-default callout-note callout-titled">
    <div class="callout-header"><div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
    <div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">No recombination detected</div></div>
    <div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
    <p>Gubbins completed but detected <strong>0 recombination blocks</strong>.
    No SNPs were masked.</p>
    </div></div>
    """))
    display(HTML(gubbins_stats.head(10).to_html(index=False, border=0,
        classes='table table-sm table-striped', justify='left')))
else:
    display(HTML("<p><em>Gubbins was not run or outputs not found.</em></p>"))
```
